text: tried to make risotto and somehow created cement. my pot may never recover
my sister still brings up the time i cried at olive garden in 2009
just described my gap year as a "strategic pivot" in an interview lol
the grocery store at 11pm is the only honest place left
i dress for the weather i want not the weather i have
had 4 espressos before noon and i can hear colors now
my dog made friends at the park and i just stood there awkwardly. again
sunday nights should be classified as a medical event
wrote a poem on a napkin at dennys and honestly it slaps
i paired boxed wine with dinosaur nuggets and called it a pairing
speedran every possible thing that could go wrong today. new personal best
burned grilled cheese so bad the smoke alarm filed a complaint
interviewer asked where i see myself in 5 years and i just stared
moved apartments and found a sock from 2019 behind the fridge
72 degrees is NOT shorts weather and i will die on this hill
the person in front of me at self checkout is scanning each grape individually
remember when you could just be a kid and not have a personal brand
humblebrag gone wrong: told my date i own property. its a timeshare
my brother ate my clearly labeled leftovers and expects forgiveness
rain is just sky soup. i will not be taking questions
put salt instead of sugar in my coffee this morning. day ruined by 7am
my moving boxes have been "temporary" for 8 months now
saw someone buying 15 rotisserie chickens at costco. respect honestly
told the interviewer my weakness is perfectionism and we both knew i was lying
accidentally made eye contact with someone at the dog park. now we talk every day
wind chill is just propaganda to make you wear a jacket
cooked a full meal for the first time and only cried twice
my sister got promoted and my mom sent ME a condolence text
just realized ive been pronouncing quinoa wrong for a decade
the vending machine at work is my only reliable coworker
i miss when the scariest thing was the ps2 red screen
interview tip: do NOT tell them you learned leadership from group projects
my anxiety did a double feature today. morning panic AND evening dread
the guy at the deli counter remembered my order. i have peaked socially
85 degrees and people acting like its the surface of the sun
found my old myspace page and honestly that person had more confidence than me
my uber driver during my move asked if i was fleeing the country
i meal prepped on sunday and by wednesday im eating cereal again
my younger brother is taller than me now and brings it up constantly
wrote "detail oriented" on my resume with a typo in it
the grocery store samples lady is the most powerful person alive
45 degrees is perfect weather and i refuse to explain myself
decaf is just coffee cosplay
accidentally liked my interviewers linkedin post from 2017 the night before
my dog ignored me at the park to hang with a golden retriever. betrayal
sundays after 5pm should have their own timezone called dread standard time
wrote something beautiful on a napkin then spilled coffee on it. art is pain
paired instant ramen with a 2019 sparkling water. notes of regret
every time i open my laptop on sunday night a little part of me dies
convinced my coworker that synergy is a real word and not just corporate poetry
my sister remembers every embarrassing thing ive done since 2003. all of them
overheard someone at trader joes say "this is MY olive oil store" and felt that
the movers broke my ikea shelf and honestly it was already broken spiritually
set 5 alarms and still somehow woke up in a panic at noon
remember when the worst notification was a farmville request
tried to bbq in the rain because i already told people i was grilling
i autopiloted to my old apartment after moving. sat in the parking lot confused
my coffee maker is the only thing in my life that performs consistently
at the interview they said "we're like a family" and i almost ran
someone at the dog park called my dog "mid" and i havent recovered
self checkout yelling UNEXPECTED ITEM like i dont have enough anxiety already
made soup from scratch. it tasted like hot water with ambition
my brother and i are in our 30s and still argue about who sits shotgun
wrote my two weeks notice on a napkin. the poetry of quitting
humidity is violence and i stand by that
told someone i wake up at 5am to journal. i wake up at 5am to panic
the old lady in produce who squeezes every avocado is my nemesis
my anxiety has a better work ethic than me tbh
miss when the hardest decision was which club penguin puffle to adopt
they asked for 3 references and i almost listed my therapist
snow is just rain that decided to be dramatic
the sunday scaries start on friday now. theyve evolved
my sister screenshot my bad take from 2016 and sends it annually
bought a $3 wine and it tasted like $3 wine. shocked
i wrote a haiku about parking tickets on the back of one
tried to fry an egg and created modern art on the ceiling
my plants died while i was moving. they chose peace
switched to oat milk and now i wont shut up about it apparently
the parking lot at aldi on saturday is a warzone and i love it
wore a suit to a zoom interview from the waist up. pajamas below
my dog has more friends than me and thats not even a joke
people who enjoy mondays are not to be trusted
remember recording songs off the radio onto cassette tapes. that was real effort
my brother got the good genes and i got whatever this is
foggy mornings are just the earth buffering
panicked at self checkout and accidentally bought 7 of the same yogurt
described my studio apartment as "cozy and efficient" on the listing. its tiny
the cheese aisle at whole foods is where i go to feel something
put "fluent in excel" on my resume. i can do SUM and thats it
paired microwave popcorn with tap water. somelier notes: buttery, hydrating
scribbled my best idea ever on a receipt. washed it in my jeans
cooked for my date and set off the smoke alarm twice. there was no second date
spring is just winter lying to your face
my cubicle plant is the only living thing that depends on me and its dying
every time i pack a box i find something i thought i lost in 2020
three shots of espresso in and i finally feel like a normal human
my older sister still introduces me as "the accident" at family dinners
someone returned a single banana at the store today. what happened to that banana
told the interviewer i thrive under pressure. i cry under pressure
47 degrees and sunny is gods apology for winter
